title: The Angel Who Calls You by Name — Henry Corbin on Avicenna's Visionary Recital
author: Henry Corbin / Avicenna

body.text: Corbin's Avicenna is an invitation — a mode of reading where philosophy and visionary experience become inseparable. Avicenna's recitals are maps of consciousness. Landscapes belonging to the mundus imaginalis, where the soul encounters realities beyond the physical and the merely subjective.
The recital opens with the appearance of the Stranger and the Guide who leads the exile toward the Orient.
Guide appears before any doctrine. The order is deliberate — the soul begins with encounter, with concepts. Wisdom arrives as a person before it becomes an idea. First meeting, then meaning.

body.caption: The Stranger meets the Guide — encounter before doctrine.

body.text: Ta'wīl is not merely interpretation but the return of the soul to its own truth.

Ta'wil transforms interpretation into ascent. The image remains visible, but it becomes increasingly transparent — the soul passes through it toward the reality that generated it. Every authentic symbol stays alive. Inexhaustible by explanation. A symbol is a door that keeps opening.
The Orient toward which the traveller moves is the place where the soul remembers.
Orient is where light rises within being itself — the visionary journey moves toward the soul's own dawn. Spiritual geography replaces terrestrial geography.

body.caption: The Orient of the soul — where light rises within being itself.

body.text: Every soul possesses a celestial counterpart toward which it develops.

Corbin's angelology transforms popular supernaturalism. The angel is a messenger visiting from elsewhere — it is guide, heavenly archetype, and future self gathered into one relationship. Individuation occurs through fidelity to this encounter. The soul meets the image of its own completed reality — the self it was always becoming. Encounter transforms. Then fidelity.
The bird begins its flight toward the homeland beyond exile.

Bird gathers everything together — the Guide has shown the way, the symbols have become transparent. Flight is escape from the world. It is the recovery of the world's hidden depth. Every genuine journey through the imaginal returns the soul toward its own origin. The homeland was inside all along.
